title: AWS Static Hosting Architecture
summary: This website is intentionally built as a secure, static, data-driven React application to demonstrate modern AWS hosting, CDN caching, private S3 origins, and CI/CD deployment patterns using CloudFront, Route53, ACM, and GitHub Actions with OIDC authentication.
requestFlow: Route53 resolves the domain to the CloudFront distribution using Alias A/AAAA records
The browser sends an HTTPS request to CloudFront using the TLS certificate provided by ACM
CloudFront serves cached content directly from edge locations whenever possible
On cache miss, CloudFront sends a signed origin request to the private S3 bucket using Origin Access Control (OAC)
S3 validates the signed request and returns the requested files to CloudFront
CloudFront caches the origin response and serves the application to the visitor over HTTPS
The React application dynamically loads JSON content from the /data directory and renders the portfolio UI

components.name: Amazon S3
components.purpose: Stores the built Vite/React application assets and runtime JSON content files inside a private S3 bucket.

components.name: Amazon CloudFront
components.purpose: Acts as the global CDN layer providing HTTPS termination, edge caching, SPA routing behavior, and secure access to the private S3 origin.

components.name: Amazon Route53
components.purpose: Hosts the zhenwei.dev DNS zone and maps the domain to the CloudFront distribution using Alias records.

components.name: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)
components.purpose: Provides and automatically renews the TLS certificate used by CloudFront for HTTPS traffic.

components.name: Origin Access Control (OAC)
components.purpose: Allows CloudFront to securely access the private S3 bucket using signed SigV4 origin requests.

components.name: S3 Bucket Policy + Public Access Block
components.purpose: Ensures the S3 bucket remains private and only allows access from the configured CloudFront distribution.

components.name: GitHub Actions
components.purpose: Runs deployment workflows for either full application deployments or data-only updates.

components.name: OIDC + IAM Deploy Role
components.purpose: Allows GitHub Actions to securely authenticate and deploy to AWS without storing long-lived AWS access keys.

pipelineDemo.title: Current Deployment Flow
steps: Application code or JSON content files are updated in the GitHub repository
A GitHub Actions workflow is manually triggered with deploy_type set to either app or data
GitHub Actions authenticates to AWS using OIDC federation and assumes the GitHub deployment IAM role
The workflow uploads or syncs the generated build assets or JSON content files to the private S3 bucket
Cache-Control headers are applied differently depending on content type to optimize browser and CDN caching
CloudFront cache invalidation is triggered for either the entire site or specific paths such as /data/*
CloudFront edge locations begin serving the updated application or content globally over HTTPS
